data.title: Polyplastics creates 2 new DURAFIDE PPS grades with 40% recycled content - rG-PPS 1140A1R00 - using mechanically recycled material.
data.description: In Tokyo on January 28, 2026, Polyplastics Co., Ltd., a renowned player in engineering thermoplastics, has introduced two fresh DURAFIDE(R) polyphenylene sulfide (PPS) variants derived from mechanically recycled materials. These grades, comprising 40% glass fiber reinforcement, are named DURAFIDE(R) rG-PPS 1140A1R00...

data.title: ARMAF introduces Odyssey Mandarin Sky Vintage fragrance at Cosmoprof Miami 2026, evoking nostalgia and reflection.
data.description: In Miami on January 28, 2026, ARMAF reintroduced the nostalgic fragrance Odyssey Mandarin Sky Vintage, which is making a comeback after being absent for a period of time. This scent embodies a sense of familiarity while also presenting a more profound and contemplative experience. The reissued fragrance offers a blend that has been shaped by the passing years, aiming to provide a fresh perspective on a classic favorite.

data.title: Thomas & Friends introduces new logo and refreshed look for Thomas the Tank Engine, part of a full-franchise relaunch.
data.description: In El Segundo, California, Thomas & Friends™ introduces a fresh new appearance for Thomas the Tank Engine as part of a complete franchise makeover set to launch this autumn.

data.title: Tripadvisor reveals 1 billion+ reviews show meaningful travel experiences driving trip planning in 2026.
data.description: A recent analysis of over 1 billion reviews and contributions reveals that experiences play a significant role in travel planning. Released by Tripadvisor®, the leading travel guidance platform, the second annual Trendcast report was unveiled on Jan. 28, 2026 in Needham, Massachusetts. This report delves into trends shaping the travel industry, providing valuable insights for travelers and businesses. With over 1 billion reviews analyzed, the report offers a comprehensive look at key factors influencing travel decisions.

data.title: American Lung Association's report warns of cutting federal tobacco prevention funds, urging states to step in.
data.description: The American Lung Association has published its 24th annual "State of Tobacco Control" report, highlighting the need for states to take action to safeguard their citizens amid a lack of progress at the federal level. The report emphasizes the urgency for states to address tobacco control issues independently. Key points from the report indicate a concerning trend, with a significant federal reduction in tobacco control efforts. This emphasizes the crucial role that states play in protecting public health. The report serves as a clarion call, urging states to bolster their efforts to combat tobacco use and related health risks.
